steps.title: Compare captive and wild settings
steps.content: The passage says captive primate studies show more right-handedness than wild primate studies. The blank follows "therefore," so it should explain why the captive setting might produce more right-handed behavior.

steps.title: Explain the captivity effect
steps.content: A and C focus on sample size, which does not explain why captivity would matter. B points to different criteria between two studies, not to the captive-wild difference. The answer is D.
